desc:
This dim tunnel is unfinished and unadorned, the walls still scarred by the
picks of Dwarven miners. Facing the entrance is a huge wooden worktable, made of
a single huge slab of oak, which also serves as a counter. Behind it, in the
distance, bright red and yellow coals glow fiercely under the great smelting
pots. Wood smoke laced with the smells of melted rock and hot metal wafts over
the counter whenever a current of air passes this way.
name: Vig's Shop

desc:
The lowest level of the city begins here, where the houses and parks of the
upper areas give way to business and government. The walls rise up straight and
proud to the ceiling, well above your head, which is covered with an intricate
pattern of stonework. The lines weave in and out and over and under each other,
leading your eyes down the hall until the tracery vanishes out of sight. The
main passage to the upper levels rises upwards and westwards, while a short
corridor going straight west leads to a doorway from which a warm blast of acrid
fumes occasionally issues.
name: Kizdin Crafts

desc:
Here all sorts of pottery is created. On the east wall sit several storage sheds
containing various types of clays. In the centre sit two potter's wheels for
forming plates and bowls and other useful objects. Finally the items are fired
in the kilns along the west wall. To the south is arrayed a selection of toys
made from wood and clay.
name: Craftsmen's Shop

desc:
Bales of wool are brought here from the upper levels where they are bought and
sold. A host of dwarves works diligently to produce cloth for their people.
First the wool is sifted through and all rocks and twigs removed. Next the
shaggy clumps are loaded onto the distaff, and spun into long strands of yarn,
spindles of which are brought to the back of the room.
name: Wool-Workers

desc:
This is a small, cramped chamber brightly lit by several wall-lanterns. The room
is filled by several looms at each of which sits a nimble dwarf, weaving the
yarn which is periodically brought from the front of the room. There is a
continuous clickety-clattering as the wooden looms are opened and closed.
name: Weavers

desc:
After a long but gentle decline from the west, the corridor here levels out.
This is the commercial centre of the city, and on either side you see doorways
to offices and workshops. The walls are lined with ornamental columns, and
between each two a simple scene is carved into the wall.
name: Kizdin Crafts

desc:
The smooth scent of leather fills the air. This room delves back far from the
main passage. In the back are vats of soaking hides, being prepared for tanning.
Nearby is a series of tanning vats slowly turning the hides into leather. There
are also a few Dwarves pounding various oils into fresh hides, producing a soft
chamois leather. Finally the leather is cut and shaped, sewn and decorated, at
tables arrayed across the front of the chamber. All types of leather goods can
be seen in various stages of production.
name: Leatherworkers

desc:
Here are the offices of those who direct the Dwarves' great work. This is a
long, somewhat narrow room, filled with orderly desks, and more paper than
tools. The light is bright and diffuse. All the tunnelling and digging and
carving is overseen from this room. The design of the caves is a careful match
of the needs of the growing population of the caves with the natural desires of
the stone in which they live.
name: Miners and Architects

desc:
All around you busy Dwarves do the work which keep this city alive. You are
completely surrounded by bustling activity, and the Dwarves pay your sloth no
mind. The passage continues east and west as far as you can see, small cones of
light dwindling off into the distance.
name: Kizdin Crafts

desc:
Here is where the miners of the area leave and pick up various mining tools.
Wooden ore buckets and picks of all sizes adorn both the walls and the floor. It
seems messy, yet the workers you see coming and going seem to know exactly where
everything is.
name: Miner's Supply Room

desc:
This dim tunnel leads deeper down into the mountain. Unlike those to the north
it is unfinished and unadorned, the wall still scarred by the picks of Dwarven
miners, whose activity you can faintly hear to the south.
name: Felak-Azan

desc:
The stolid hall stretches off before you. To the east the decor changes; the
patterns of stonework end, and a wide variety of axes hang upon the walls.
Around you are bearded Dwarves of every description and station. Nobility in
their finery walk quickly east to the King's court, while labourers walk the
same way for a pint of mead in the Khazad Kheled-Baruk. On one side of you a
cart is pulled, stacked high with cloths and thread in a thousand colours, and
behind that comes a mule loaded with pig iron. Anything your hands or stomach or
head needs must surely be close at hand.
name: Kizdin Crafts

desc:
Here a master cobbler and several journeymen produce most of the footwear for
the city. There is a variety of materials spread about the workbenches, but
leather and cotton seem to predominate. The work here is quiet and relaxed, and
a joking banter passes back and forth amongst the cobblers. You see that several
guilds have set up their shop here. In the immediate area you notice that the
woolworkers and a tailor have set up a table.
name: Cobblers Market

desc:
This room is filled with a wide variety of toys, all beautiful and some
obviously magical. You see puppets and soft cloth dolls, metal soldiers, wooden
animals, hobbyhorses, small clay ducks on wheels, miniature boats, intricately
carved tops, and all sorts of puzzles and tricks. A few Dwarves sit over
cluttered workbenches, adding to the stocks.
name: Toymakers' Workshop

desc:
The tunnel here is only dimly lit by lanterns along the wall. This seems to be a
recent excavation, and is as yet unused. To the south you hear the sound of
picks striking stone wafting up from a downward-sloping tunnel.
name: Felak-Azan

desc:
From the pick marks on the walls around you, this passage was purposefully
delved, but it seems to have been abandoned. Several dark lanterns hang from the
walls.
name: Felak-Narag

desc:
A spiral staircase has been carved into the stone, winding its way around and
around some fifty feet up and down. A single painting covers the outside wall, a
broad overview of some distant battle which the Dwarves will never forget, and
never forgive.
name: Top of a Spiral Staircase

desc:
A small line of houses extends off from the main route. The rock changes as the
tunnel extends to the south, where it is no longer suitable for mining.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This sweltering room contains the Dwarven ironworks. Carts are wheeled up to the
entrance, where they deposit their ore in large bins. In this part of the
chamber sturdy Dwarves crush the ore into small pieces. Nothing is wasted; the
iron dust is sintered into larger, more useful lumps. Carts bring the bits of
iron ore south, where the heat intensifies.
name: Ironworks

desc:
Groups of sweating, bare-chested Dwarves labour in the heat to process the
steady flow of iron ore. In the centre of this area is a cylindrical stone-
built furnace. Into it the Dwarves feed a mixture of coal and iron ore. Around
the furnace stand Dwarves pumping stolidly on tuyeres, feeding the flames
within. After a time they remove a solid lump of malleable iron, adding it to
the stacks on the side of the room. This cavern is near the top of the mountain,
and several large ventilation shafts rise from the ceiling.
name: Ironworks

desc:
You are within an enormous cavern which extends beyond your vision and whose
ceiling is lost in darkness. Though the floor is perfectly smooth, the walls are
rough, filled with small niches and rocky knobs sticking into the hall. To your
northeast, in the centre of this vast chamber, you can see what appear to be
mountains rising up from the floor. Nearest you is a grey mount, rising up to a
silver peak. Farther north stands one like a tooth tipped with snow; its walls
glowing red in the cavern's light.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
You are within an enormous cavern which extends beyond your vision and whose
ceiling is lost in darkness. Though the floor is perfectly smooth, the walls are
rough, filled with small niches and protrusions sticking into the hall. Directly
to the north you see a miniature mountain rising high above your head, even
here, deep within the Blue Mountains. It rises quickly, hard grey stone
cunningly crafted by the Dwarves, with a silver peak gleaming in the cave's
light.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
You stand within an enormous cavern which extends beyond your vision and whose
ceiling is lost in darkness. Though the floor is perfectly smooth, the walls are
rough, filled with small niches and rocky knobs sticking into the hall. Just to
the east a vast tower of rock rises from the floor, gleaming a snow- white
silver at its peak. To the northeast stands a mountainous tower with sheer,
dull-red sides. Peeking between them you can just see the white peak of a third
tower.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
You are standing in a magnificent hall, the walls are filled with all sorts of
things collected from near and far. To the east there is a sturdy oakdoor which
leads out from this home, south of here there is another chamber.
name: The Entrance

desc:
The walls of this chamber are very smooth and if it wasnt already known there
would almost not be any way of telling this is a chamber dug out of the stone.
The floor is made of marble and is very carefully polished almost making it
possible to see reflections in it. To the west a velved passage leads into a
small back-chamber which probably is the bed chamber of the owner of this
beautyful home. North of here is an entrance hall.
name: An Inner Chamber

desc:
This is a great circular pit carved in the native stone of the mountains. A
great staircase, wide enough for twenty dwarves to walk abreast, rises gradually
upward, turning counter-clockwise as it goes. Its risers are paved with marble,
and black onyx sconces on the wall hold lamps which are continually filled from
an unseen source and kept burning.
name: Base of a Spiral Staircase

desc:
The hall descends slightly as it continues to the west, and the simple homes
seem to become a bit more prosperous. Many Dwarves amble along here, as in the
rest of this hall, but you see almost no children. The people of Mahal are
strong and hardy, but still, as the long years pass, they diminish.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This is a large, grand hall with a curved ceiling rising far above your head.
All along the walls extend hundreds of Dwarven apartments, built into the living
rock, rising into the darkness. All along these living quarters, balconies
extend, and Dwarves walk around on them, or from apartment to apartment, or
along the floor where you stand. This seems to be a prosperous area, dotted with
marble pillars and elegant statuary. The cavern extends to the west and north.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This is a large, grand hall with a curved ceiling rising far above your head.
All along the walls extend hundreds of Dwarven apartments, built into the living
rock, rising into the darkness. All along these living quarters, balconies
extend, and Dwarves walk around on them, or from apartment to apartment, or
along the floor where you stand. This seems to be a prosperous area, dotted with
marble pillars and elegant statuary. The cavern extends to the east and north.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This is a large, grand hall with a curved ceiling rising far above your head.
All along the walls extend hundreds of Dwarven apartments, built into the living
rock, rising into the darkness. All along these living quarters, balconies
extend, and Dwarves walk around on them, or from apartment to apartment, or
along the floor where you stand. This seems to be a prosperous area, dotted with
marble pillars and elegant statuary. The cavern extends to the west and south.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This is a large, grand hall with a curved ceiling rising far above your head.
All along the walls extend hundreds of Dwarven apartments, built into the living
rock, rising into the darkness. All along these living quarters, balconies
extend, and Dwarves walk around on them, or from apartment to apartment, or
along the floor where you stand. This seems to be a prosperous area, dotted with
marble pillars and elegant statuary. The cavern extends east and south.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
There is a small stretch of apartments here, in a passage between two large
caverns. To the west the rows of Dwarven doorways abruptly ends where a new
tunnel is being delved.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
This rocky tunnel is still being excavated, but already a few hardy Dwarves are
claiming it for their homes. There is a small vein of silver running through the
mountains here, and the Dwarves mine it carefully. Carts of iron ore are also
frequently wheeled away to the blast furnace.
name: Kizdin-Zirak

desc:
Starting from the mouth of a tunnel to the east, it appears that many dwarves
have already chosen this place to make their homes. A marble hall, though its
construction is kept very simple, glimmers brightly when answering the
reflection of the burning flame from the torches that are hung on the walls.
name: A Marble Hall

desc:
The marble hall takes a turn northwards, as it leads to the dwarven homes that
are built deep inside the mountain. Torches line the way, their light flickering
and dancing on the walls. Most times, the faint sounds of mining and falling
rocks can be heard echoing off the walls.
name: A Marble Hall

desc:
The marble walkway takes yet another turn, leading deeper into the stone-laid
dwarven tunnels. The polished marble glitters in return to any light source that
passes, and echoes hollowly upon every step taken. Most times sounds of further
digging echo off the walls. It seems as if the dwarves here seldom rest.
name: A Marble Hall

desc:
The marble tunnel comes to a stop at an intersection of excavations. To all
directions but east lie sites for new dwarven homes, carefully channeled into
the stone by the busy dwarven builders of the Blue Mountains. A few piles of
flint and rocks litter the floor of the hall, indicating that the construction
here is at the final stage.
name: A Marble Hall

desc:
This rocky landing has a smooth stony floor. It looks as if the dwarven builders
have started to work on this vacant lot in order to make it more suitable for a
comfortable home. Some spots on the wall have been reinforced with polished
stone plates. The rest lies bare, waiting for the touches of its new habitant.
name: An Excavated Landing

desc:
The landing rises up from the marble hall, a set of stone stairs carved in
between, probably for easier access. The landing has been cleaned up, apart from
a lone mine-cart that stands in the corner, half-filled with rubble and stones.
It looks as this arched stone tunnel could be easily arranged to fit to a
dwarfs' liking, providing them with a comfortable home.
name: An Excavated Landing

desc:
The large hall is filled with large tables with all sort of miniature mining
equipment placed on them. On one of the tables small carts are placed on some
sort of railway, while miniatures of dwarves are equipped with small picks,
hammers and tongs.
name: Gabildum of the Miniatures

desc:
In the rough cut walls small sparkles of silver reflect the light coming from
the large hall to the north. A huge wooden desk, with a silver plated top, is
placed in the centre of the hall. An unfinished picture of mining equipment is
placed on the desk. Cabinets and racks with bound sheets of paper are placed
along the walls.
name: Kibilgundu

desc:
This small oval shaped hall has rich decorated walls; gems, each with a label
attached are placed on one of the shelves at the left wall, while several maps,
with marks of dwarf mining activity are attached to the opposite wall. At the
far end of the hall you notice a bed made of solid granite and a couple of
chests. One of them has dirty clothes hanging out of it.
name: Francis' Gundu

desc:
Tall obsidian pillars rise from the dark floor of this ominous hall and stretch
to the curved ceiling above. Glowing runes woven with silver and gold adorn the
pillars, narrating Iror's many adventures and triumphs. Rising from the centre
of the hall is an obsidian throne, so dark it appears to absorb the light which
fills the hall from two roaring fireplaces. Flanking the throne are many niches
delved between the pillars consisting of Iror's personal library, bed chambers,
and other rooms. A lectern stands in front of the throne, with a hastily
scribbled scroll tossed upon it...
name: Iror's Private Halls

desc:
The room has been carved from granite, now smooth like glass. A fireplace in the
southern part heats the room. A bed sits against the western wall, and to the
east a small desk and a chair. A sconce above the desk holds a lit torch, giving
light to the workspace. A small sign has been nailed to one of the walls. A
small ventilation shaft can be seen high up in the north-eastern corner of the
ceiling, leading in air which streams out through the roaring fireplace, giving
the room good ventilation. The granite floor is slightly heated from the grand
fireplace.
name: Nainn's Quarters

desc:
Here the miners have come upon a natural opening in the rock. The floors have
been polished smooth recently, and the walls bedecked with paintings and some
tapestries, in preparation of new Dwarven Homes.
name: Felak-Azan

desc:
Lamps mounted on the smooth stone walls cast flickering shadows around this
well-lit hall. Every surface is impeccably clean, and polished to a shine. The
three columns along the north wall, and the three columns along the south wall
each depict one of the nameless six Dwarven fathers. Above the eastern doorway
the seventh Dwarven father, Durin the first, the deathless, king of Khazad-dum
is shown wearing a crown of seven stars. In the centre of the room rests a
massive stone table around which visitors to Fenuln's hall can gather and feast.
name: Fenuln's Hall

desc:
The walls of this bedroom are rough cut granite with patches of green malachite
carefully exposed, but still embedded in the walls where the water dripping
through the copper rich stone naturally deposited the mineral. Between the large
black bear fur covering the floor and the roaring fireplace, the chamber is
quite cosy. The room is exceptionally well ventilated as there is barely any
scent of smoke from the fire, and a gentle breeze of fresh air can be felt. In
the corner a stone staircase leads downwards.
name: Malachite Bedroom

desc:
Stalactites hang from the roof of the natural portions of the cavern, and broken
rocks show the obvious signs of excavation elsewhere. A faint dripping sound can
be heard, as the water from the nearby lake filters through the mountain. Piles
of discarded stone are stacked adjacent to where valuable ore has been hewn from
the mine. Although the chunks of granite look haphazardly cracked, a closer
inspection reveals that the fractures must have been masterfully placed to
efficiently extract all of the ore with the least number of pickaxe strikes.
name: Rocky Excavation

desc:
Though Dwarves by their nature are not drawn to stealth, some skills have found
their way into Dwarven usage. Here, within mighty Zirakzigil many Dwarves hone
these skills, trained by formidable rogue-captains. Though the Dwarves refuse
such skills as backstabbing, all manner of other rogue skills you see practiced
here.
name: Zirakzigil

desc:
The natural walls here have been carved and chiseled with an extraordinary
pattern that resembles a brick wall. A large forge stands along the western
wall, ablaze and venting through the ceiling above. Tool racks along the eastern
wall are neatly kept and well organized. A large mural was beautifully painted
near the forge of seven well-formed dwarves that are sleeping peacefully, while
a craftsmen guards them protectively. The glow from the forge compliments this
mural to honour Aule and the Seven Fathers.
name: Shrine of Aule

desc:
This bed chamber is has a very relaxing atmosphere, along one wall there is a
magnificent bed made out of sturdy oak-wood and over it is lays a bedspread made
out of the finest furs. Several very interesting metal pieces and other trophies
are hanging from the walls and standing in the corners, the owner of this home
must be out of the ordinary, even for being a dwarf.
name: A Cosy Bed Chamber
